# Page Title: Biosciences and Bioengineering | IIT Jammu

Department Vision

In line with the institute's vision, the department's goals focus on:

1. Pursuing impactful, world-class research in distinct areas for achieving excellence.
2. Providing state-of-the-art teaching and training aimed at bioengineering-based product realization.
3. Pursuing high-end interdisciplinary research through national and global collaborations.
4. Addressing regional and national challenges offering sustainable solutions.
5. Engaging in community outreach to popularize science to improve human health and environmental wellness.

#### Gaurav Ashok Bhaduri

An enthusiastic and self-motivated researcher and academic. My vision is "Innovation with sustainability for society". Me and my research group look towards energy effective, bio-inspired, biochemical processes for solving current socitial problems. This is done by involving chemical engineering and biological science principles for process development using energy efficient intensification techniques.

##### Research Interest

Carbon capture storage and utilization (CCSU), Process Intensification, Environmental Engineering, Biochemical and Bioprocess Engineering, Waste to wealth, Nanomaterial synthesis and characterization, Photochemistry and Photocatalysis, Plasmonics, X-ray photoelectron spectroscopy, Heterogeneous catalysis (tri-phasic reactions), Nanofluids, Process design, Teaching and Learning in Higher Education (TLHE).

##### Education Qualification

PhD: University of Newcastle upon Tyne (Newcastle University), UK (2015)

BE (Chemical), University of Pune (Now Savitribai Phule Pune University), Pune (2009)

##### Academic Interests

As an academic most of our time is dedicated towards students learning. Classroom engagement is an important aspect of teaching. I work on development of new techniques of class room teaching. This is based on the epistemology represented in Indian thought and the mythological construct of the subcontinent. Within this theme we are focusing on many different aspect of Engineering Education

1. Active classroom engagement
2. Development of indigenous pedagogy techniques for engineering education
3. Active laboratory engagement and assessment
4. Innovations in summative assessment

##### Teaching Engagements

I have taught various course at the UG, PG and PhD level in Chemical Engieering and other desiplines (such as Bioscience and Bioengineering and Humanities and Social Sciences).

#### Jigmi Basumatary

Dr. Jigmi Basumatary has been an Assistant Professor in the Department of Biosciences and Bioengineering at the Indian Institute of Technology Jammu since February 2026. His research focuses on the development and advancement of diverse optical imaging and manipulation tools for studying biological systems.

##### Research Interest

Dr. Jigmi’s research interests include key technologies such as single-molecule localization microscopy (SMLM), light-sheet microscopy, optical tweezers, and photoacoustic/ultrasound imaging. He adopts a multidisciplinary approach that integrates optical engineering with other science and engineering disciplines, along with rigorous qualitative and quantitative analysis. The overarching goal of his research is to develop and extend imaging techniques to address fundamental biological questions.

- Mithu Baidya
- Assistant Professor
- mithu.baidya@iitjammu.ac.in

#### Mithu Baidya

Our research focuses on membrane proteins, particularly G protein-coupled receptors (GPCRs). Our primary goal is to unravel the molecular basis of GPCR function and leverage this knowledge to develop improved medicines for the future. Our research aims to enhance our understanding of membrane proteins and their role in cellular communication, paving the way for targeted and effective therapeutic interventions.

Lab site: **https://sites.google.com/view/receptorbiologylaboratory**

##### Research Interest

The Receptor Biology laboratory at IIT Jammu focuses on understanding the molecular mechanisms of G protein-coupled receptors (GPCRs) in physiological and pathophysiological processes. GPCRs play a vital role as mediators of cellular signaling and are the largest receptor superfamily encoded by the human genome. Recognizing their involvement in numerous biological functions and their significance as drug targets, the lab aims to unravel the intricate workings of GPCRs at the molecular level to develop safer and more effective therapeutics.

To achieve this, the lab employs a range of research techniques, including biochemical, biophysical, chemogenetic, and pharmacological approaches. By utilizing multiple methods, we strive to comprehensively understand GPCR function and its molecular interactions and signaling pathways.

The lab's research themes focus on two key areas. Firstly, to investigate the molecular basis of interaction and modulation between GPCRs and pore-forming toxins. The insight into toxin influence on GPCRs can yield underappreciated signaling responses and immunomodulatory effects on our physiology. A holistic understanding of the dynamics of toxin-receptor interaction and signaling will open up new opportunities to develop novel therapeutic interventions.

Secondly, the lab is dedicated to unraveling the mechanisms behind signaling bias in GPCRs. GPCR biasing involves selectively activating or inhibiting specific signaling pathways downstream of a GPCR while sparing others. GPCR biasing holds great promise for developing next-generation therapeutics with enhanced efficacy and reduced side effects. Our continuing research effort in this direction is expected to uncover new insights into GPCR signaling and facilitate the design of novel drugs that can selectively modulate specific signaling pathways, leading to more precise and personalized treatments for various diseases.

##### Education Qualification

- PhD, Indian Institute of Technology Kharagpur, 2016

##### Professional Background

2016-04 - 2023-01: Postdoctoral Fellow, Indian Institute of Technology Kanpur

##### Academic Interests

**Research Areas**

Area: G protein-coupled receptor (GPCR) biology and Cell signaling

Approaches: Biochemical, biophysical and chemogenetic based methods.

**Research Interests**

G protein-coupled receptors (GPCRs) act as membrane gatekeepers and are the pleiotropic mediators of cellular signaling. GPCRs are the largest receptor superfamily in the human genome, and it plays indispensable roles in almost all the known physiological processes. As a result, almost half of the currently prescribed drugs work by targeting GPCRs. Our lab’s long-term focus is to understand the underlying molecular basis of GPCR functioning in many physiological and pathophysiological processes. We employ multiple orthogonal and combinatorial approaches ranging from biochemical, biophysical, chemogenetic, and pharmacological approaches in our studies. Leveraging this molecular information will enable us to design better and safer GPCR therapeutic interventions.

Current research themes:

1. Molecular basis of interaction and modulation by the pore-forming toxins on GPCRs.

2. Molecular function of arrestin fold proteins in protozoan parasites.

3. Mechanism of β-arrestin bias in G protein-coupled receptors for designing

next-generation drugs with reduced side effects.

For details, please visit the lab webpage: **https://sites.google.com/view/receptorbiologylaboratory**

**Teaching Interests**

Genetic Engineering, Protein Biochemistry, Molecular cell biology, Molecular Pharmacology, Molecular Parasitology

##### Research Interest

**Goal**

Our lab at IIT Jammu focuses on understanding the complexities of female reproductive tissues, particularly in the context of gynecological diseases and cancers. This research emphasizes the significance of both coding and noncoding regulatory elements in the genome (REGULOME), which drive cellular heterogeneity and plasticity. By employing advanced Next-Generation Sequencing (NGS) techniques and computational models, we aim to map these regulatory elements to uncover mechanisms underlying disease progression and treatment resistance. The ultimate goal is to develop targeted therapies that address tissue heterogeneity and improve women's reproductive health.

#### Shirsendu Ghosh

Dr. Shirsendu Ghosh did his graduation from Jadavpur University with honors in Chemistry (2009). Then he obtained his master’s degree in Chemistry from IIT Kharagpur (2011). He received his Ph.D. degree from the Indian Association for the Cultivation of Science (IACS), Kolkata in 2016. After a Postdoctoral stint at Weizmann Institute of Science, Israel (2016-2021) he joined as a research associate at Cornell University, USA (2021-2022). After that, he served as an Assistant Professor in the Dept. of Chemistry, GITAM Hyderabad for two years (2022-2024). Then he joined as an Assistant Professor in the Dept. of Chemistry with a joint affiliation in the Department of Bioscience and Bioengineering at IIT Jammu on December 2024. His area of research revolves around the development and use of new optical spectroscopy and microscopy techniques to decipher the dynamics of biological systems and live cells. More specifically, he developed total internal reflection fluorescence microscopy-based super-resolution microscopic techniques to investigate the role of plasma membrane topography in cancer biology and immunology (such as T-cell signaling pathways and Mast cell signaling pathways). He is also interested in deciphering the consequences of long-range charge reorganization within a protein on the allosteric signaling of proteins which may lead to the development of a pathway to control allosteric signaling. He employs single-molecule fluorescence microscopy and time-resolved confocal microscopy techniques to decipher the changes in polarity, viscosity, and dynamics of different regions of biomolecules (such as proteins, DNA, lipid vesicles, etc.) and live cells. He is also interested in the development of low-cost protein-coated copper nanoclusters for drug delivery and ion sensing. He is the recipient of the prestigious Ramanujan Fellowship (SERB), DST-inspired faculty fellowship, Koshland Prize (Koshland Foundation, Israel), and CSIR-Research Fellowship.

#### Vivek B Raina

The general theme of our research focuses on answering the fundamental question of how cells deal with DNA damage, particularly DNA double-strand breaks —one of the most critical threats to genomic integrity..

We use a combination of cutting-edge single-molecule DNA curtains technology along with biochemical, genetic, and cell biological approaches to mechanistically understand how DNA repair pathways safeguard genomic stability. A key aspect of our work also explores how these DNA repair mechanisms are finely tuned during gamete formation, ensuring faithful transmission of genetic information across generations.

# Page Title: Biosciences and Bioengineering | IIT Jammu

### M. Tech in Biosciences and Bioengineering with  Specialization in Medical Devices

The field of biomedical engineering has seen tremendous growth over the past few decades, with advancements in technology leading to the development of new medical devices, diagnostic tools, and therapeutic interventions. At the same time, the realm of tissue engineering and regenerative medicine in healthcare is rapidly expanding, offering new opportunities to improve patient care and outcomes. To address the need for professionals who can bridge the gap between technology and healthcare, the Department of Biosciences and Bioengineering, IIT Jammu proposes to offer a two-year postgraduate program viz. Master of Technology with specialization in Biomedical Devices. The program will be a trans-disciplinary one, with active participation of faculty besides from the core department, BSBE, from the departments of electrical engineering, mechanical engineering, chemical engineering and materials engineering. The program aims to provide students with a comprehensive understanding of the field of biomedical engineering and medical devices, including advanced training to make them Industry-ready by inclusion of industry-specific component. Students will be equipped with advanced technical knowledge and skills to address real-world problems in the field of medical devices and biomedical engineering which shall prepare them for careers in research and development, the healthcare industry, and academia. The program also aims to train students to apply their knowledge and skills to develop innovative solutions that enhance patient care, improve healthcare outcomes, and advance medical science.

Department Vision

In line with the institute's vision, the department's goals focus on:

1. Pursuing impactful, world-class research in distinct areas for achieving excellence.
2. Providing state-of-the-art teaching and training aimed at bioengineering-based product realization.
3. Pursuing high-end interdisciplinary research through national and global collaborations.
4. Addressing regional and national challenges offering sustainable solutions.
5. Engaging in community outreach to popularize science to improve human health and environmental wellness.
